spec_match.rationale: The demo now constructs real PaymentRequest objects for secure-payment-confirmation, calls canMakePayment(), and uses current SPC fields including instrument.details, showOptOut, and paymentEntitiesLogos.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised editable amount, currency, payee, instrument, logo, timeout, opt-out, icon, credential, scenario, confirm, fallback, and cancel controls with visible request and dialog updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now frames the Chrome 139 UX refresh as an unchanged Payment Request API surface with richer dialog fields and clearer merchant outcome handling.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces success, continue-without-SPC, cancellation, authentication failure, unsupported-browser fallback, and invalid non-HTTPS payeeOrigin constructor failure.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the required ChromeStatus URL plus W3C Secure Payment Confirmation and Chrome for Developers SPC references.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, paired button colors, no source inline style attributes, data URLs for demo logos, and textContent/DOM construction for dynamic output.

summary: The SPC UX Demo open questions are closed. DevTools MCP verified real SPC PaymentRequest construction and canMakePayment(), edited dialog fields with two payment entity logos, success, continue-without-SPC, cancel, authentication-failed, invalid payeeOrigin, unsupported-browser, explicit fallback, and explicit cancel branches. The refreshed conformance route passed 10/10 assertions with clean console and network output.
